outlook.headline: Hardware-assisted verification demand stays hot with tight supply
outlook.summary: Demand is broadening and still running hot, led by Synopsys’ hyperscaler and top-tier semiconductor customer pull and Cadence’s Palladium Z3 gaining share with long lead times. AMD’s strength in test, measurement, emulation, and defense suggests the demand backdrop remains healthy, while MKS points to supportive volume and mix across adjacent equipment spend. Watch for whether tight availability persists or if order growth cools after this quarter’s strong momentum.

drivers.label: Hyperscaler and leading semiconductor demand
drivers.detail: Strong end-customer pull is driving continued growth in hardware-assisted verification spending.

drivers.label: Competitive displacement in emulation
drivers.detail: Palladium Z3 is described as the gold standard, with competitive displacement and long lead times indicating demand is outpacing supply.

drivers.label: Healthy test/measurement/emulation activity
drivers.detail: Embedded revenue strength in test, measurement, emulation, and defense points to a solid demand environment for verification-related products.

drivers.label: Supportive broader equipment demand
drivers.detail: Higher volume and favorable mix, alongside 22% chemistry revenue growth, suggest a constructive spending backdrop across related tooling.

risks: Any easing in hyperscaler or leading semiconductor customer demand could quickly slow growth.
Lead times may normalize if supply catches up, reducing the scarcity-driven tailwind.
Adjacencies like test/measurement and broader equipment spend could soften if end-market budgets tighten.
